definition.name: multiplicity_decomposition
definition.definition: Let π : A → B(H) be a *-representation of a *-algebra A with finite-dimensional commutant (equivalently, the face F_{S(A)}(φ) is finite-dimensional for the associated state). Then π decomposes as a direct sum π ≅ ⊕_{j=1}^k σ_j^{⊕n_j}, where σ_1, ..., σ_k are pairwise unitarily inequivalent irreducible representations and n_j ∈ N are the multiplicities (the number of times σ_j appears). The commutant then decomposes as π(A)' ≅ ⊕_{j=1}^k M_{n_j}(C), a direct sum of full matrix algebras, and consequently dim_R(π(A)'^{sa}) = Σ_{j=1}^k n_j². The integer k counts the number of distinct irreducible types (superselection sectors in physics), while n_j measures the degeneracy of each type. This is a standard consequence of the Artin-Wedderburn theorem applied to the finite-dimensional von Neumann algebra π(A)'. Reference: Takesaki, Theory of Operator Algebras I, §I.11.
